name: Qalam Education Fund
mission:
Inspiring growth through nurturing education built on a sacred foundation.

We envision a community that thrives off of accessible Islamic knowledge and...
theoryOfChange: Qalam’s approach is to inspire growth through nurturing education built on a sacred foundation, connecting what they impart back to the Holy Qur’an and the Prophetic tradition, with a three-pronged approach: Ta'leem (learning of sacred knowledge), Tarbiya (personal growth and development), and Suhba (mentorship and companionship).
scoreSummary: Qalam Education Fund Inc shows good alignment and moderate impact, with zakat compliance (major risk deductions applied).
zakatClaimEvidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://www.qalam.institute/zakat (Source: https://www.qalam.institute/zakat)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Context dependent profile. Consider cause fit, risk profile, and evidence maturity before deciding.

keyConcerns.headline: Zakat-collecting organization holds 110 months of reserves
keyConcerns.detail: Zakat must be distributed promptly per Islamic jurisprudence. Large reserves raise concerns about timely distribution.

components.evidence: Explicit zakat program (+4); Muslim-focused organization (+2); Islamic identity (+1)
components.improvement_suggestion: Clarify zakat allocation policy and eligible use categories for donors.

alignment.rationale: Alignment 31/50: MEDIUM Muslim donor fit, education global cause area

components.evidence: No beneficiary data available for cost-per-beneficiary scoring; good program ratio (≥75%) suggests reasonable delivery
components.improvement_suggestion: Publish beneficiary counts consistently so cost-effectiveness can be assessed.
components.name: Cost Per Beneficiary

components.improvement_suggestion: Publish a time-bound plan to deploy excess unrestricted reserves into mission delivery, while keeping a clear operating reserve floor.
components.name: Financial Health

judge_issues.message: The impact rationale mentions a lack of publicly available cost-per-beneficiary data, which is accurate. However, it fails to mention the significant deduction due to excessive working capital (109.7 months), which heavily impacted the impact score. The small board size (2 members) is also a key factor that should be mentioned.

dimension_explanations.alignment: The organization shows medium alignment for Muslim donors by providing Zakat-eligible educational opportunities and addressing global needs for Islamic literacy [2][7]. The alignment score of 31/50 highlights its established track record in the education sector, though donor fit is categorized as medium [5].
dimension_explanations.impact: Qalam Education Fund Inc reported a total revenue of $2,329,468 and maintains a program expense ratio of 81.2%, reflecting a commitment to its educational mission [1][3]. While the organization supports significant capacity building through its seminary and digital media, the direct impact score of 24/50 reflects a lack of publicly available cost-per-beneficiary data [4][6].

baseline_narrative.headline: Qalam Education Fund Inc provides accessible Islamic knowledge through comprehensive educational programs and a sacred foundation.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 81.2%, indicating a strong focus on direct service delivery
Exceptional fundraising efficiency with a cost of only $0.01 per $1 raised
Robust liquidity with 109.7 months of working capital
baseline_narrative.summary: Qalam Education Fund Inc is a non-profit organization dedicated to nurturing community growth through programs such as the Qalam Seminary, the Qalam Podcast, and the Qur’an Intensive [4][5]. The organization is Zakat-eligible, maintaining a specific policy to distribute funds according to established asnaf categories [2][7].

alignment.explanation: Strong alignment for Muslim donors due to <cite id="2">explicit zakat programs</cite> and a focus on <cite id="1">high-urgency global education</cite>.
alignment.improvement: Clarify the <cite id="2">specific zakat allocation policy</cite> and eligible use categories to provide more detail to donors.

credibility.explanation: While the organization is <cite id="1">verified as Zakat-eligible</cite> and has a long <cite id="1">17-year track record</cite>, its credibility is hampered by a <cite id="3">small board of 2 members</cite> and a lack of published transparency seals.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the charity should expand its board to at least 5 independent members and <cite id="8">adopt formal effectiveness policies</cite> as recommended by the BBB.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who value long-term Islamic institutional building and want to support a rapidly growing educational platform with high financial efficiency.

Impact is measured through educational reach rather than poverty metrics

impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that educational programs are effectively translating into community growth and leadership development.

strengths.detail: The organization maintains a <cite id="1">81.2% program expense ratio</cite>, ensuring that resources are focused on educational delivery.
Qalam spends only <cite id="1">$0.01 to raise every $1</cite>, which is significantly better than industry benchmarks.
The charity holds <cite id="1">109.7 months of working capital</cite>, providing immense long-term financial stability.

strengths_deep_dive: Qalam's financial health is characterized by rapid growth and lean operations. The organization achieved a <cite id="3">3-year revenue CAGR of 568.6%</cite>, growing from modest beginnings to a multi-million dollar institution. This growth is supported by a <cite id="3">total asset base of $11,717,936</cite>, which includes significant investments in its physical and digital infrastructure. Its grantmaking activity also supports the broader ecosystem, with <cite id="3">$414,289 in total grants</cite> distributed to domestic partners like Roots Community Inc and the Qalam Seminary.
rich_narrative.summary: Qalam Education Fund Inc is a Texas-based educational institution <cite id="1">founded in 2009</cite> with a mission to provide accessible Islamic knowledge through a sacred foundation. The organization operates a diverse suite of programs including the <cite id="4">Qalam Seminary, the Qalam Podcast, and the Qur’an Intensive</cite>, which focus on spiritual development and community leadership. Financially, the organization reported <cite id="3">total revenue of $2,338,320</cite> in the 2023 tax year, reflecting significant growth in its capacity to serve the Muslim community. A hallmark of its financial model is its <cite id="1">81.2% program expense ratio</cite>, suggesting that all reported functional expenses are directed toward its educational mission. Furthermore, the organization demonstrates <cite id="1">exceptional fundraising efficiency</cite>, spending only <cite id="1">$0.01 per $1 raised</cite>. Beyond its core campus activities, Qalam maintains a global reach through digital media and <cite id="4">travel retreats to locations like Costa Rica</cite>, ensuring that Islamic education remains accessible to a modern, mobile audience.
